reviewer.name: OpenAI Codex
reviewer.responsibility: Claim extraction, primary-source mapping, scope correction, and verification design

sources.title: JSON
sources.versionScope: Current ECMAScript JSON object and JSON syntax

claims.statement: JSON is a text syntax for serialized data, distinct from JavaScript object values.
claims.applicability: Current ECMAScript JSON object; JSON data syntax

claims.statement: JSON.stringify serializes supported values and JSON.parse parses valid JSON text.
claims.applicability: Current ECMAScript JSON object; JSON data syntax

claims.statement: Invalid JSON can throw and some JavaScript values do not round-trip unchanged.
claims.applicability: Current ECMAScript JSON object; JSON data syntax

verifications.method: Stringify a known object, parse the text, and compare the round-tripped data.
Try invalid JSON and confirm the parse failure is handled or reported at the boundary.
verifications.expectedResult: The parse failure is handled or reported at the boundary.

verifications.method: Inspect the stored or transported text and confirm it contains the intended serializable fields.
verifications.expectedResult: It contains the intended serializable fields.

limitations: JSON is not the same thing as a JavaScript object, even though their surface syntax overlaps.
JSON.parse throws on invalid JSON text unless the caller catches the error.
Not every JavaScript value round-trips through JSON: unsupported values may be omitted, changed, or rejected.
Independent human review remains required before promotion to gold.
